channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - New York Knicks
channel.description: New York Knicks trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: OG Anunoby expected to be ready for Game 1 of Eastern Conference Finals
item.description: Knicks Videos: "OG Anunoby will play in Game 1. I feel very comfortable saying that, just because of where this thing was in the day or two days after it happened." On The Putback with @IanBegley , Ian says that barring any setbacks, the Knicks should expect to have OG Anunoby ready to go for Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals

item.title: Front office maintaining asking price of "a young …
item.description: Underdog NBA: Latest on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade front, per @ShamsCharania : - Front office maintaining asking price of "a young blue-chip talent and/or a surplus of draft picks" - Bucks also leaving the door open to pursue a major trade to convince Giannis to stay once again - Wolves, Cavs, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers pursued Giannis at the trade deadline

item.title: The altar was here. The casket was there. There were …
item.description: In her dining room, Kathleen Reinhard can still see the tall teenager at her son’s funeral service in 2012. She is wearing a dark New York Knicks hoodie while sitting near a tray of her homemade banana bread, describing the church scene by pointing to different spots on her table. The altar was here. The casket was there. There were rows of pews here and here, and Karl-Anthony Towns was right over there. Reinhard didn’t know who Towns was on that winter morning inside St. Agnes Church in Clark,

item.title: “For all the accomplishments I had, it meant nothing …
item.description: Towns was also a St. Joseph of Metuchen class president who thought he should be present for a fallen alum, a 25-year-old member of the United States Marine Corps who was among six killed in a helicopter crash while supporting combat operations in Afghanistan. Kevin Reinhard was once a strong 6-foot-4 volleyball player on a powerhouse St. Joe’s team, and Towns was overwhelmed by the outpouring of affection and respect for him. At 16, he was already used to being celebrated in print. “I grew up i

item.title: “They’re screaming for me to shoot,” he recalled. But …
item.description: Towns had to do something, anything, to honor a Marine he had never met. So the following day, he scored 25 varsity points against Perth Amboy — one for every year Reinhard spent on the planet — and then shut it down. “I just stopped shooting,” Towns recalled. His grandmother lived in Perth Amboy, and his entire family was in the stands, wanting him to add on to his new career high. “They’re screaming for me to shoot,” he recalled. But Towns was done looking at the basket, he said, “because I ha

item.title: “It was the darkest time of my life when Kevin was …
item.description: Another 6-foot-10 St. Joe’s player, James Ziemba, recalled being stunned by his younger friend’s maturity and awareness. Towns had quietly laid out his plan before tipoff. “It’s a nice thing to want to do,” Ziemba said, “but then to actually do it, as a freshman? “That was Karl. He always wanted to be part of that community. It wasn’t just a place for him to pass through and move on.” The Reinhard family wasn’t aware of this small act of kindness until they talked to school officials about start

item.title: Timberwolves, Cavaliers, Celtics, Knicks, Lakers could pursue Giannis Antetokounmpo depending on how their playoffs go
item.description: The NBA playoffs represent another landmark point for the Antetokounmpo sweepstakes. Sources said postseason teams such as the Minnesota Timberwolves, Cleveland Cavaliers, Boston Celtics, Knicks and Los Angeles Lakers pursued Antetokounmpo at the February deadline, and their finishes to the campaign will play a major factor in their aggressiveness to trade for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Largest point differential during a seven-game win …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Largest point differential during a seven-game win streak in the playoffs in NBA history: 1. 2026 Knicks: +185 . . . . . . . . . . . . 2. 1985 Lakers: +133 3. 2017 Cavs: +131 4. 2010 Magic: +129 5. 2026 Thunder: +128 6. 1986 Celtics: +127

item.title: The New York Knicks have outscored their first and …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: So far in the 2026 NBA Playoffs, the New York Knicks have outscored their first and second round opponents by 194 points, the largest margin in league history over any 10-game postseason span. Previous mark of 171 points was set by the Golden State Warriors in 2017. (via @statitudes.com ) #NBA

item.title: Josh Hart: I used to think Philly was a sports town, I don't know if it is anymore
item.description: [SNY] Josh Hart says he wasn't surprised at how many Knicks fans were in Philly: "I used to think Philly was a sports town, I don't know if it is anymore"

item.title: It's significantly cheaper to go down to Philly and …
item.description: Yaron Weitzman: It's the prices. Or, more specifically, how insanely expensive it is to go to MSG. That's it. It's significantly cheaper to go down to Philly and watch a game than to go the Garden. For Lots of Knicks fans, this is the only way they can even dream of going to a Knicks playoff game.

item.title: Tyrese Maxey on Knicks fans taking over the 76ers arena: ‘It absolutely sucks’
item.description: Derek Bodner: Tyrese Maxey on the Knicks fan takeover in Philly: "It absolutely sucks. It just sucks. That's really all I can say about it, man...There's only one way to put a stop to it: we have to go out there and win these games. It felt louder here for them than it did in the Garden."

item.title: Joel Embiid, on the Knicks: "At times, it's okay to …
item.description: Derek Bodner: Joel Embiid, on the Knicks: "At times, it's okay to just say the other team was better....Gotta get better, from top to bottom. Ownership, front office, players, coaches. Everybody just gotta get better."

item.title: Miles McBride on reaching back-to-back Eastern …
item.description: Ian Begley: Miles McBride on reaching back-to-back Eastern Conference Finals: “That’s what’s expected… that’s not the goal.”

item.title: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with …
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are the 4th team in NBA history with multiple 30-point series-clinching wins in a single postseason. They join the 2025 Thunder, 2008 Celtics, and 1987 Lakers. All 3 of those teams went on to win the NBA title.

item.title: Knicks sweep Philadelphia in lights-out shooting game
item.description: Tim Bontemps: Final: Knicks 144, Sixers 114 An absolute demolition by the Knicks, who sweep Philly, pick up a seventh consecutive victory overall & advance to the Eastern Conference finals for a second straight year. Philly has not advanced to the conference finals for a quarter century.

item.title: Knicks just tied the NBA playoff record for most …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Knicks just tied the NBA playoff record for most 3-pointers in a game.

item.title: Best Knicks playoff series ever in point differential: …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Best Knicks playoff series ever in point differential: 2026 vs. Atlanta: +105. 2026 vs. Philadelphia: +89. Yes, their two best marks in NBA history have happened in this postseason

item.title: Knicks will sweep a best-of-7 series for the first …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks will sweep a best-of-7 series for the first time since 1999 & the third time in team history. Last time the Knicks swept a series, they went to the Finals...

item.title: The Knicks will now have at least a week off until the …
item.description: Adam Zagoria: The Knicks will now have at least a week off until the conference finals. The earliest they would play Game 1 would be next Sunday, per Mike Breen.

item.title: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Knicks with 18 threes in the first half, tied for the most in a playoff half in NBA history. Most since the Cavs in 2016.

item.title: Knicks fans heckiling Joel Embiid at the free throw …
item.description: CJ Fogler: Knicks fans heckiling Joel Embiid at the free throw line in Philly

item.title: Knicks tie playoff record for most three-pointers in a quarter
item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are rolling early. They have tied the NBA playoff record for most 3-pt FG in any quarter (11).

item.title: This crowd is overwhelmingly -- I mean overwhemingly …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: This crowd is overwhelmingly -- I mean overwhemingly -- Knicks fans. It's really like a game at MSG.

item.title: OG Anunoby out vs. Sixers
item.description: The Knicks are playing it cautiously. OG Anunoby (right hamstring strain) is out for their Game 4 clash against the 76ers Sunday afternoon at Xfinity Mobile Arena as they look to finish off a series sweep. He also missed the Knicks’ Game 3 win on Friday with the injury. He is still considered day-to-day and the Knicks have been optimistic that he won’t miss much time. Imaging revealed that it is a “very minor” strain, The Post’s Stefan Bondy previously reported.

item.title: Brown shares that OG Anunoby is out for today's Game 4 …
item.description: Knicks Videos: Mike Brown shares that OG Anunoby is out for today's Game 4 matchup with the 76ers:

item.description: Knicks Videos: "OG Anunoby will play in Game 1. I feel very comfortable saying that, just because of where this thing was in the day or two days after it happened." On The Putback with @IanBegley , Ian says that barring any setbacks, the Knicks should expect to have OG Anunoby ready to go for Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals

item.description: ESPN Insights: The Knicks are rolling early. They have tied the NBA playoff record for most 3-pt FG in any quarter (11).

item.title: OG Anunoby out vs. Sixers
item.description: The Knicks are playing it cautiously. OG Anunoby (right hamstring strain) is out for their Game 4 clash against the 76ers Sunday afternoon at Xfinity Mobile Arena as they look to finish off a series sweep. He also missed the Knicks’ Game 3 win on Friday with the injury. He is still considered day-to-day and the Knicks have been optimistic that he won’t miss much time. Imaging revealed that it is a “very minor” strain, The Post’s Stefan Bondy previously reported.

item.title: Brown shares that OG Anunoby is out for today's Game 4 …
item.description: Knicks Videos: Mike Brown shares that OG Anunoby is out for today's Game 4 matchup with the 76ers:

item.title: From the latest Physicality / Playing Hard report: …
item.description: Roland Beech: From the latest Physicality / Playing Hard report: Knicks-Sixers... Jalen dominated the rookie

item.title: Prior to the playoffs, the most-watched game of the …
item.description: While some of the credit may belong to Anthony Edwards and the Timberwolves — who have played in highly-viewed games in each of their past two playoff runs — the Spurs account for several high water marks this season. Prior to the playoffs, the most-watched game of the season was Spurs-Thunder on ABC/ESPN Christmas Day and the most-watched outside of broadcast television was the Knicks-Spurs NBA Cup Final on Prime.

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns, an avid card collector, revealed …
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns, an avid card collector, revealed that he was outbid for a one-of-one Randy Orton WrestleMania 41 patch autograph. The winning bid came in at $42,100 on eBay. “This has us SICK,” Towns wrote on his Instagram account “bigbodegascards,” which is dedicated to his card collecting. “Thought it was ours for the taking, until a last-minute NUCLEAR bid snuck in! Who has this card now!? We have to know!”

item.title: OG Anunoby 'a go' for East Finals
item.description: Shams Charania: The latest for @SportsCenter on optimism for New York's OG Anunoby entering the Eastern Conference finals and details on Bob Myers leading the search for the 76ers new top executive: “Sources tell me that OG Anunoby, there's an expectation that he will be able to play in game one of the Eastern Conference Finals, barring any setbacks. There's optimism for his status for game one today, OG Anunoby participated in parts of practice. He also had an individual on court workout. He's

item.title: Jalen Brunson: We have our plan. Obviously utilize …
item.description: Jalen Brunson: We have our plan. Obviously utilize rest, but then when we're in the gym, no, we're doing everything we have to do to stay in rhythm. Stay having the edge that we need to have. Focusing on the things that we need to focus on and continuing to prepare. Obviously, we have to prepare for um one of these two teams and then we've talked about the differences between the two and all that, but just focusing on what makes us the best thing we can be as well.

item.title: I was blessed with it not being a fracture and …
item.description: You got hurt. Four guys got hurt. Do you ever let yourself think like maybe luck is on our side this time? This wasn't as bad as it felt. Josh Hart: No, I mean, no, I don't really believe in the luck. For me personally I pray before every game in terms of protection for myself, my team, and I just go out there and hoop, when I had the hand, I'm just I obviously walked back and got X-rays of that. I was just I'm sitting there praying about it. I mean, that's my first go-to. just sitting there pra
